_comment: SYNTHETIC maintenance knowledge for the causality engine. Fictional manual pages + technician checks, keyed by failure_mode id. Committed test data (Evidence class 3) -- no licensed or real-vendor content. These are the citable 'manual pages' MIRA references in an explanation.

manual_refs.snippet: A fouled or misaligned photoeye latches a permanent 'product present' signal, halting the feed logic.
Wipe the lens; verify the indicator LED toggles when the target clears.

checks: Visually inspect the photoeye lens for product debris, condensation, or label adhesive.
Clear the target and confirm the sensor output toggles (LED off).
Verify alignment to the reflector / confirm sensing distance.
Check the photoeye wiring/connector for damage.

manual_refs.snippet: Rising drive current with no outfeed indicates a mechanical jam; clear before resetting the drive.

checks: Lock out the drive, then inspect the belt/chain path for a jammed product or foreign object.
Check for a seized roller or failed bearing (spin by hand under LOTO).
Inspect the drive for an overcurrent fault code before resetting.

manual_refs.snippet: The drive will not start until the enable input and run permissive are both true and no fault is latched.

checks: Confirm the drive Enable input is energized.
Verify no latched drive fault (clear/reset if present and safe).
Check the run permissive chain (e-stop, guards, upstream ready).

manual_refs.snippet: Repeated overcurrent trips indicate a binding load or a degrading motor/bearing.

checks: Read the overload relay / drive trip code and the last current peak.
Under LOTO, rotate the shaft by hand to feel for binding or bearing roughness.
Verify the load is free (no jam) before resetting the overload.

manual_refs.snippet: Compare the live process value to a reference; recalibrate if drift exceeds the tolerance band.

checks: Compare the sensor reading to an independent reference/handheld.
Inspect the sensor for coating, scaling, or a loose fitting.
Recalibrate per the SOP and re-verify against the reference.

manual_refs.snippet: Header pressure below spec causes sluggish actuators plant-wide; check compressor, dryer, and leaks.

checks: Read the air header pressure gauge against the spec (e.g. 85-100 psi).
Check the compressor run state and the dryer fault status.
Walk the line for an audible leak or a stuck/dumping actuator.

manual_refs.snippet: An open or faulted interlock inhibits start and holds the machine in a safe state.

checks: Identify which guard/interlock reads open on the safety status.
Confirm the guard is physically closed and the switch actuates.
Inspect the interlock switch and wiring for damage (follow safety procedure).

manual_refs.snippet: Lost device comms presents as stale tags and a held machine state; check link, address, and switch.

checks: Confirm the device link/activity LEDs and ping/whois the device.
Check for a duplicate address or a failed switch port.
Verify the driver/connection status in the gateway.
